title: Growth hormone JAK-STAT (Turner syndrome)
vignette: A 6-year-old girl is brought to the office for evaluation of short stature. Her parents have noticed that she bears little resemblance to her 2 older siblings. The patient's mother and father are 172.5 cm (5'8") and 183 cm (6') tall, respectively. On physical examination, the patient's height is 3 standard deviations below the mean for her age. Other findings include low-set ears, a high arched palate, a webbed neck, and cubitus valgus. Chromosomal analysis reveals a 45,XO karyotype. The patient is started on a medication to improve growth and normalize her height. Which of the following intracellular pathways is stimulated by the medication used in this patient?

clues.note: Short stature 3 SD below the mean is the chief complaint and the treatment target. The medication chosen to fix it is what the question is really testing.

clues.text: low-set ears, a high arched palate, a webbed neck, and cubitus valgus
clues.note: This dysmorphology cluster (webbed neck, cubitus valgus, low-set ears, high-arched palate) is classic Turner syndrome. It steers you to a 45,XO girl.
A 45,XO karyotype confirms Turner syndrome. Turner-related growth failure is treated with recombinant human growth hormone.

clues.text: medication to improve growth and normalize her height
clues.note: The drug is recombinant GROWTH HORMONE. The question asks for ITS intracellular signaling pathway — growth hormone (a peptide) signals through cell-surface receptors and JAK-STAT.

whatMatters: Chain it: Turner syndrome (45,XO + webbed neck/cubitus valgus) -> treated with growth hormone -> name growth hormone's signaling pathway. The disease is just the route to the drug; the testable point is that GH (and prolactin, cytokines, EPO, G-CSF) act through the JAK-STAT pathway. Answer E.
strategy: Classify hormones by signaling: JAK-STAT (growth hormone, prolactin, cytokines, EPO, G-CSF, leptin); cAMP/PKA via Gs (glucagon, PTH, ACTH, ADH-V2, FSH/LH/TSH, beta-agonists); IP3/DAG via Gq (GnRH, TRH, angiotensin II, ADH-V1, alpha-1); intracellular DNA-binding receptors (steroids, thyroid, vitamin D); receptor tyrosine kinases (insulin, IGF, growth factors).

options.text: Binding of activated receptors to DNA to modify transcription

eliminations.label: intracellular DNA-binding receptor = steroid/thyroid
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: the mechanism of STEROID and THYROID hormones — lipophilic ligands bind intracellular receptors that act directly on DNA to modify transcription. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: growth hormone is a peptide using a cell-SURFACE receptor (JAK-STAT), not an intracellular DNA-binding receptor. ✅ Correct if: cortisol, aldosterone, estrogen, testosterone, T3/T4, or vitamin D.

eliminations.label: cAMP-PKA (Gs) — not GH's pathway (your pick)
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: Gs-coupled receptors raise cAMP and activate protein kinase A (e.g., glucagon, PTH, ACTH, ADH-V2, beta-agonists). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: GH does not signal through cAMP/PKA; it dimerizes its receptor and activates JAK2-STAT. The trap is defaulting to the most familiar second messenger. ✅ Correct if: a Gs-protein hormone (glucagon, PTH, ACTH, FSH/LH/TSH, ADH-V2).

eliminations.label: DAG-PKC (Gq) — wrong hormone class
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: Gq-coupled receptors activate phospholipase C -> DAG -> protein kinase C. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: GH isn't a Gq hormone, so DAG/PKC isn't its pathway. ✅ Correct if: a Gq hormone (GnRH, TRH, angiotensin II, ADH-V1, alpha-1 agonists).

eliminations.label: IP3-calcium-calmodulin (Gq) — wrong hormone class
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: the other Gq arm — phospholipase C makes IP3, releasing Ca2+ that acts via calmodulin. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: same Gq family, not used by growth hormone. ✅ Correct if: a Gq hormone driving IP3/Ca2+ signaling.

reasoning: Short stature + webbed neck + cubitus valgus + low-set ears + 45,XO = Turner syndrome
Turner-related growth failure is treated with recombinant human GROWTH HORMONE
Growth hormone binds a cell-surface receptor that dimerizes and signals through JAK2-STAT (inducing IGF-1)
mechanism: The clinical picture (short stature, webbed neck, cubitus valgus, low-set ears, 45,XO) is Turner syndrome, treated with recombinant human growth hormone to normalize height. Growth hormone binds a single-pass cell-surface receptor; one GH molecule bridges two receptors, causing dimerization and activation of receptor-associated Janus kinase (JAK2), a nonreceptor tyrosine kinase. JAK2 phosphorylates tyrosine residues that dock STAT proteins; phosphorylated STATs dimerize, translocate to the nucleus, and induce transcription of IGF-1 and other growth genes. Cytokines (e.g., interferon), erythropoietin, G-CSF, and prolactin use the same JAK-STAT pathway.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Biochem/Cell > signaling by receptor class: cAMP/PKA (Gs), IP3/DAG (Gq), JAK-STAT (GH, prolactin, cytokines, EPO, G-CSF), intracellular/steroid receptors (steroid, thyroid, vitamin D), RTKs (insulin, IGF). Plus Turner syndrome features and GH therapy.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: Two steps: diagnose Turner (45,XO + features) -> the drug is growth hormone -> recall GH's pathway (JAK-STAT). The question tests the DRUG's signaling, not the disease.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on JAK-STAT, Janus kinase, STAT, IGF-1, second messengers (cAMP, IP3, DAG), cubitus valgus, 45,XO? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: cAMP-PKA (B) is the trap — it's the most-memorized hormone pathway, an easy default. But GH uses JAK-STAT, not cAMP.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank the JAK-STAT users: Growth hormone, Prolactin, cytokines/interferons, EPO, G-CSF, thrombopoietin, leptin.

keywords.front: Short stature + webbed neck + cubitus valgus + 45,XO

keywords.back: Glucagon, PTH, ACTH, ADH-V2, FSH/LH/TSH, beta-agonists
GnRH, TRH, angiotensin II, ADH-V1, alpha-1

firstAid.detail: Biochem/Cell > GH/prolactin/cytokines/EPO/G-CSF use JAK-STAT -> IGF-1; receptor dimerization, JAK2, STAT phosphorylation.
firstAid.location: Biochem/Cell > GH/prolactin/cytokines/EPO/G-CSF use JAK-STAT -> IGF-1; receptor dimerization, JAK2, STAT phosphorylation.

firstAid.detail: Cell > cAMP/PKA (Gs), DAG-PKC and IP3-Ca2+ (Gq), intracellular/steroid receptors, receptor tyrosine kinases (insulin/IGF).
firstAid.location: Cell > cAMP/PKA (Gs), DAG-PKC and IP3-Ca2+ (Gq), intracellular/steroid receptors, receptor tyrosine kinases (insulin/IGF).

firstAid.detail: Repro/Genetics > 45,XO: short stature, webbed neck, cubitus valgus, streak ovaries, coarctation/bicuspid valve; GH + estrogen therapy.
firstAid.location: Repro/Genetics > 45,XO: short stature, webbed neck, cubitus valgus, streak ovaries, coarctation/bicuspid valve; GH + estrogen therapy.

memoryHook: GH, Prolactin, EPO, G-CSF, and cytokines all JACK each other up the STATs (JAK-STAT). The Turner girl gets growth hormone -> JAK-STAT.
